hook: Tagesschau and The Punch — opposite political universes — both said the same thing about Hantavirus outbreak on MV Hondius. Nobody else did.

tweets.text: 31 buckets, 55 articles, 7 distinct frames in today's coverage.

Frame: RISK_REASSURANCE_PARADOX (3 buckets). "The World Health Organization said Thursday that more hantavirus cases could emerge after the disease killed three passengers from a cruise…" — Standard Kenya World
Frame: TRANSMISSION_RARITY_DEBATE (2 buckets). "WHO confirms Andes strain of hantavirus in cruise ship passengers, with 3 transferred from ship for treatment" — CBC World
Frame: ORIGIN_UNCERTAINTY (2 buckets). "Todos los extranjeros que viajan en el barco serán repatriados a sus respectivos países cuando lleguen a las islas Canarias, incluso si pre…" — Clarin
Frame: MEDICAL_IMPROVISATION_HEROISM (2 buckets). "Hantavirus, il passeggero Usa che è diventato medico di bordo" — Il Sole 24 Ore

The paradox: Urgent-containment-focused (Germany contact-tracing escalation) and reassurance-focused (Nigeria/WHO low-risk messaging) outlets converge on the necessity for rigorous isolation and rapid repatriation—the only shared operational truth across divergent risk framings.…
Silence as data: `brazil` didn't cover this — they covered Minimal coverage (33 tokens); regional South American origin context subordinated to WHO coordination narratives. instead.
**Italy - Il Sole 24 Ore** (italy): American physician-passenger (Oregon doctor Stephen Kornfeld) becomes de facto ship's medical director after the ship's doctor falls ill.
